text:
## 📋 Summary

New TypeScript module that builds Schema.org `Product` and `BreadcrumbList` JSON-LD for a catalogue page. The two builders are clean and well-typed, but `brandName` contains a logic bug: it filters suppliers by the presence of a `url` even though only `name` is ever consumed. Because of this, suppliers with a valid brand name but no URL are silently discarded, and the code falls back to `categoryLabel` for the brand. A test would have caught this immediately.

## 🔍 Findings

- 🟠 **High — `brandName` filters on the wrong field (`hard_product_jsonld.ts:30`).**
  The filter `s.url ?? ""` and the regex `/^https?:\/\//i` gate on whether the supplier has a usable outbound link, but the function only reads `suppliers[0].name`. Consequences:
  - If the first supplier has a `name` but no `url`, it is skipped and the next URL-bearing supplier (or the category fallback) is used.
  - If no supplier has a `url` but some have `name`, the code returns `categoryLabel` as the brand — wrong product metadata emitted to search engines.
  The comment on the file explicitly calls out that "the suppliers list is kept ONLY to resolve the brand name", which makes the URL check the wrong thing to be checking.
  **Fix:** filter on what is actually consumed, e.g.
  ```ts
  const suppliers = product.suppliers.filter((s) => s.name && s.name.trim().length > 0);
  if (suppliers.length > 0) return suppliers[0].name;
  return product.categoryLabel;
  ```
  Even simpler, since only the first non-empty name is needed:
  ```ts
  const named = product.suppliers.find((s) => s.name?.trim());
  return named ? named.name : product.categoryLabel;
  ```

- 🟡 **Medium — No tests alongside a new pure function with a non-trivial branch.**
  `brandName` has a fallback path, a filter, and an index into the first match — exactly the shape that wants a unit test. Please add at least: (a) suppliers with a URL but no name, (b) suppliers with a name but no URL, (c) suppliers with both, (d) empty `suppliers`. Right now there is nothing to catch a regression on the brand fallback.

- 🟡 **Medium — `image` URL is built by simple concatenation (`hard_product_jsonld.ts:47`).**
  `image: \`${SITE}${product.image}\`` silently produces a malformed URL (`https://www.example-agri.co.ukfoo.jpg`) if a caller passes a path without a leading `/`, and produces `https://www.example-agri.co.ukhttps://cdn…` if a caller passes an absolute URL. Either normalise with `new URL(product.image, SITE).toString()` (which also handles both cases correctly) or assert/document the leading-slash contract and validate at the boundary.

- 🟢 **Low — `slug` is interpolated into `url` and the breadcrumb item without escaping (`hard_product_jsonld.ts:48`, `:61`).**
  If a slug can ever contain characters like `?`, `#`, `&`, spaces, or non-ASCII text, the resulting URLs in the JSON-LD will be invalid. Consider `encodeURIComponent` for the path segment and ensuring the upstream slug source is normalised.

- 🟢 **Low — Stale `url` field on `Supplier`.**
  The interface still carries `url?: string` with a comment saying it is "no longer rendered". Since the only consumer (`brandName`) is the source of the bug and does not need it, the field can be deleted along with the fix, simplifying the type and removing the temptation to keep filtering on it.

## ✅ Positives

- Clean, minimal schema output with the correct `@context`, `@type`, and `BreadcrumbList` `position` numbering.
- `Brand` and `BreadcrumbList` shapes match Schema.org expectations.
- `Product` builder correctly uses a separate `category` slug vs. `categoryLabel` for display in the breadcrumb, avoiding slug leakage into human-readable text.
- The `// HARD fixture` header comment is a nice touch for future reviewers; it makes the intended one-bug audit tractable.
